---
title: "Quiz February 10 and 11"
author:
- name: Tom Hanna
  id: th
  orcid: 0000-0002-8054-0335
  email: tlhanna@central.uh.edu
  url: tomhanna.me
  affiliation:
  - name: University of Houston
    city: Houston
    state: TX
    url: uh.edu/class/political-science/
license: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0
copyright:
  holder: Tom Hanna
  year: 2026
date: today
format:
        html:
                theme: cosmo
                style: ../custom.scss
                axe: true
                self-contained: true
                code-fold: false
                logo: ../assets/logo.png
                footer: "POLS3316, Spring 2026, Instructor: Tom Hanna"
        revealjs:
                self-contained: true
                transition: convex
                incremental: true
                slide-number: true
                preview-links: true
                theme: ../custom.scss
                logo: ../assets/logo.png
                footer: "UH POLS3316, Spring 2026, Instructor: Tom Hanna"
        docx:
                toc: true
                number-sections: true
        pdf:
                toc: true
                toc-depth: 2
                toc-location: left
                number-sections: true
                fig_caption: true
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| eval: true
#| label: setup

library(here)
here::i_am("Quizzes/quiz_1.qmd")
```

## Instructions

Write your name here: _____________________________

1. Please read each question carefully and answer to the best of your ability.
2. You may simplify to least common denominator and to the radical expression, $\sqrt{xxx}$, if necessary.


## Question 1 - Mean

Find the mean of the following numbers: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10.

## Question 2 - Median

Find the median of the following numbers: 3, 1, 4, 1, 5.

## Question 3 - Mode

Find the mode of the following numbers: 7, 8, 7, 9, 10. 

## Question 4 - Range

Find the range of the following numbers: 12, 15, 20, 10, 18.

## Question 5 - Variance

Find the variance of the following numbers: 5, 7, 9, 11, 13.

## Question 6 - Standard Deviation

Find the standard deviation of the following numbers: 4, 6, 8, 10, 12.

## Question 7 - Mean 

Find the mean of the following numbers: 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900, 1000, 1100.

## Question 8 - Median

Find the median of the following numbers: 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900, 1000, 1100.

## Question 9 - Standard Deviation given variance

Find the standard deviation of a variable, given that the variance is 100.

## Question 10 - Variance given standard deviation

Find the variance of a variable, given that the standard deviation is 15.

## Question 11 - n-1

When finding the sample variance, we divide by n-1 instead of n. What is this called?

## Question 12 - Mode 

What is the mode of the following numbers: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10?

## Question 13 - Mode 

What is the mode of the following numbers: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5?

## Authorship and License

- Author: Tom Hanna
- Website: <a href="https://tom-hanna.org/">tomhanna.me</a>
- License: This work is licensed under a <a href="http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/">Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International License.</a>

<a href="http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/">
  <img src="../assets/creative_commons_license.png" alt="Creative Commons License Badge">
</a>
